title: Squeeze the Day: National Hugging Day
story: Every January 21, people across the country celebrate National Hugging Day by giving warm embraces to family, friends, and loved ones. This special day encourages everyone to share the simple joy of hugging, which scientists say can actually make us feel happier and less stressed. Schools often organize hug-a-thons, families have extra snuggle time, and some communities even set up "free hugs" booths in parks and town squares. Social media fills with photos of people hugging their pets, siblings, grandparents, and even their favorite stuffed animals, proving that hugs really are one of life's best free gifts.
activity_question: According to the story, what do scientists say hugging can do?
activity_choices: Make us feel happier and less stressed
activity_answer: Make us feel happier and less stressed
follow_up_question: Who in your life gives the best hugs and why?

title: Crunch Time for Granola Bars!
story: Every January 21, people across America celebrate National Granola Bar Day by munching on their favorite chewy or crunchy bars! Some folks pack homemade granola bars with oats, nuts, and dried fruit for school lunches. Others grab store-bought bars for hiking adventures or quick snacks. Many families use this day to try new flavors like chocolate chip, peanut butter, or berry blast. It's the perfect excuse to enjoy a tasty treat that's both delicious and energizing!
activity_question: What are two main textures that granola bars can have?
follow_up_question: What would you put in your perfect homemade granola bar?

title: Going Nuts for Our Furry Friends
story: Squirrels get their own special day every January 21 when people celebrate National Squirrel Appreciation Day! On this nutty holiday, families put out extra birdseed, nuts, and treats in their yards to help squirrels stay fed during winter. Kids love watching these bushy-tailed acrobats leap from tree to tree and stuff their cheeks with food. Many people share funny squirrel photos and videos online, showing off the silly antics of their backyard visitors. Some nature centers even host special squirrel-watching events where families can learn cool facts about these clever creatures and their amazing jumping skills.
activity_question: What do people put out in their yards to help squirrels on this holiday?
bonus_fact: Squirrels can jump up to 10 times their body length!

title: Say Cheese for National Cheese Lovers Day!
story: Every January 21, cheese fans across America celebrate National Cheese Lovers Day by diving into their favorite dairy delights! People host cheese-tasting parties with everything from sharp cheddar to creamy brie. Restaurants offer special cheese-filled menus, while grocery stores set up fancy cheese displays. Social media fills with photos of gooey grilled cheese sandwiches, towering cheeseburgers, and colorful cheese boards. Some families try a new cheese they've never tasted before, while others stick to classics like mozzarella on pizza or mac and cheese. It's the perfect excuse to be extra cheesy!
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Cheese Lovers Day?
activity_choices: Photos of cheese dishes and cheese boards
Pictures of their pets wearing cheese hats
Videos of cheese factories being built
activity_answer: Photos of cheese dishes and cheese boards
follow_up_question: What's the most unusual cheese combination you'd be brave enough to try?

title: Chowder Power Day!
story: Creamy, chunky soup takes center stage every January 21 during National New England Clam Chowder Day! This thick, white soup packed with tender clams, potatoes, and celery becomes the star of restaurant menus across America. People slurp bowls of this comfort food, share photos of their favorite chowder spots online, and even try making homemade versions in their kitchens.
activity_question: What ingredients are mentioned as being in New England clam chowder?
bonus_fact: New England clam chowder is white, unlike Manhattan clam chowder which is red!

title: Blonde Brownies Take the Spotlight!
story: Blonde brownies get their moment to shine every January 21! These golden treats aren't actually brownies at all – they're blondies, made with brown sugar and vanilla instead of chocolate. People celebrate by baking batches of these chewy, butterscotch-flavored squares and sharing them with friends and family. Some bakers add chocolate chips, nuts, or caramel swirls to make them extra special. Social media fills with photos of homemade blondies as people show off their favorite recipes and creative twists on this classic dessert.
activity_question: What gives blonde brownies their golden color and butterscotch flavor?
bonus_fact: Blondies were actually invented before chocolate brownies!

title: Put Pen to Paper Day!
story: Grab a pen and show off your best cursive on January 21 for National Handwriting Day! People celebrate by writing thank-you notes, practicing their signature, or creating handwritten letters to friends and family instead of typing or texting.
activity_question: What do people write on National Handwriting Day instead of typing?
follow_up_question: What would you write in a handwritten letter to your best friend?

title: Sweet Words Make Everyone's Day Brighter
story: People across the country spread kindness on January 21 by celebrating National Compliment Day! This special holiday encourages everyone to give genuine compliments to friends, family, teachers, and even strangers. You might tell your mom her cookies are amazing, compliment a classmate's cool sneakers, or thank your bus driver for their safe driving. Many people post positive messages on social media, write thank-you notes, or simply smile and say something nice to brighten someone's day. The best part? Giving compliments makes both the giver and receiver feel fantastic, creating a chain reaction of happiness that spreads throughout communities everywhere.
activity_question: What is one way people celebrate National Compliment Day?
activity_choices: By giving genuine compliments to others
activity_answer: By giving genuine compliments to others
follow_up_question: What's the best compliment you've ever received or given to someone?
